name: stack_align_at_printf_with_double
trigger: Calling printf with a double argument from a function that has an odd number of 8-byte stack slots — RSP must be 16-byte aligned at the call. Misalignment crashes inside printf's SSE path.
causal_chain: main has 3 int locals → frame layout uses ≥3 slots → if prologue does not pad to 16-byte alignment, RSP is 8 mod 16 at call → printf hits 'movaps' on misaligned stack → SIGSEGV
suggested_fix: In function prologue, after pushing rbp + saved registers, round local-area size up to keep (RSP+8) % 16 == 0 (i.e. RSP % 16 == 8 just before the call instruction, which becomes 0 after the call's implicit push of return address — wait, that's the receiving side). On the calling side: ensure RSP % 16 == 0 just before 'call printf' by padding sub rsp,N where N = round_up(locals, 16) + 8.
regression_test: this corpus entry; add variants with 1, 2, 3, 4 int locals to confirm padding is robust
notes: Reference cc compiles+runs fine. If target produces a binary that segfaults inside printf, the divergent runtime exit (target=139, reference=0) is the smoking gun.
